paper_title: Importance of the advection scheme for the simulation of water isotopes over Antarctica by atmospheric general circulation models: A case study for present-day and Last Glacial Maximum with LMDZ-iso

methods.description: This method involves using the isotopic AGCM LMDZ-iso to simulate water isotopes over Antarctica under different conditions, including present-day and Last Glacial Maximum (LGM). The method evaluates the impact of different advection schemes on the simulation results.

steps.description: Use the isotopic AGCM LMDZ-iso at a standard latitude-longitude R96 grid resolution with 39 vertical layers.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize LMDZ-iso model with R96 grid resolution and 39 vertical layers

steps.description: Perform three sensitivity simulations with LMDZ-iso under present-day conditions for the post spin-up period 1990-2008.
steps.searchable_summary: run sensitivity simulations with LMDZ-iso for 1990-2008

steps.description: Conduct control simulation with the van Leer advection scheme and two other simulations with a single upstream scheme on horizontal and vertical directions.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate with van Leer and upstream advection schemes
outputs: simulation results with different advection schemes

steps.description: Evaluate the impacts of advection schemes on the temporal relationship between water isotopic contents of precipitation and mean air surface temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: assess advection scheme impact on isotopic content and temperature relationship

steps.description: Retrieve model values at data geographical coordinates by bilinear interpolation for quantitative model-data comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate model values to data coordinates using bilinear interpolation
